Description: Eli Lilly completed its ~$1.2B acquisition of Ventyx; Ventyx shares were delisted following deal completion.
Asahi Kasei agreed to acquire Germany-based Aicuris for €780M to expand its infectious-disease/antiviral footprint.
Servier agreed to acquire Day One for $21.50/share in cash (equity value ~ $2.5B) to expand its rare oncology portfolio.
Japan's Asahi Kasei acquires Germany's AiCuris for €780M.
Ventyx Biosciences completes $1.2B acquisition by Eli Lilly; company delists from Nasdaq.
Esperion acquired Corstasis; terms include $75M cash upfront plus up to $180M in milestones (total up to $255M).
Esperion to acquire Corstasis and Enbumyst; $75M upfront cash plus up to $180M contingent payments (deal value up to ~ $255M).
J&J completed Proteologix acquisition for $850M cash with potential additional milestone payment.
Vertex agreed to acquire Alpine for $65/share, total value ~$4.9B, to add povetacicept and other immunology assets.
Gilead completed acquisition of CymaBay to add seladelpar (PBC) to its liver disease portfolio; deal valued about $4.3B.
AstraZeneca agreed to acquire Fusion to strengthen radioconjugate pipeline; offered $21/share cash plus CVR; total consideration about $2.4B.
J&J completed acquisition of Ambrx in an all-cash transaction with total equity value of about $2.0B.
Novartis agreed to acquire MorphoSys for $68 per share in cash (equity value ~$2.9B) to strengthen oncology pipeline.
Sanofi to acquire Inhibrx; shareholders receive $30 cash plus a CVR and shares in a new spun company retaining other assets.
BMS to acquire RayzeBio for $62.50/share cash, equity value ~ $4.1B.
BMS agreed to acquire Karuna for $330/share in cash (~$14B equity value) to add KarXT (xanomeline/trospium) schizophrenia asset.
AbbVie agreed to acquire Cerevel for $45/share in cash (~$8.7B) to expand neuroscience pipeline.
Roche entered merger agreement to acquire Carmot for $2.7B upfront plus up to $400M milestones.
AbbVie acquired ImmunoGen for $31.26/share in cash (~$10.1B equity value) to add Elahere ADC.
Lilly agreed to acquire POINT for $12.50/share in cash (~$1.4B) to expand radioligand therapy portfolio.
Novartis completed Chinook acquisition for $3.2B upfront; total deal value up to $3.5B including CVR.
Merck agreed to acquire Prometheus for $200/share in cash (~$10.8B) to add PRA023 (MK-7240) for ulcerative colitis/Crohn's.
Pfizer entered definitive agreement to acquire Seagen for $229/share in cash (enterprise value ~$43B) to strengthen oncology franchise.

Description: Click on an acquiring company to pull up more information, and scroll to the bottom of the page to read how this information was collected and organized. If there's anything we've missed, or any additional information you'd like to see, please reach out and let us know. Editor's note: If tables or values do not display, please try clearing your browser's cache and reloading the page. For this database, BioPharma Dive looked only at companies developing human medicines. The mergers and acquisitions included in the database are limited by certain criteria, namely that they must be for an entire company — not for product licensings, options to acquire or for partial equity stakes.

Acquirer: Additionally, the database is limited to deals valued at a minimum of $50 million upfront. Total consideration can reflect both cash and equity offered upfront in exchange for the
Target: company’s shares. Deals in which the upfront payment was less than $50 million or was not specified, like with Eli Lilly's purchase of Versanis and Orna Therapeutics or Novartis' acquisition of Kate Therapeutics, were not included.
Description: Editor's note: If tables or values do not display, please try clearing your browser's cache and reloading the page. For this database, BioPharma Dive looked only at companies developing human medicines. The mergers and acquisitions included in the database are limited by certain criteria, namely that they must be for an entire company — not for product licensings, options to acquire or for partial equity stakes. Additionally, the database is limited to deals valued at a minimum of $50 million upfront. Total consideration can reflect both cash and equity offered upfront in exchange for the acquired company’s shares. Deals in which the upfront payment was less than $50 million or was not specified, like with Eli Lilly's purchase of Versanis and Orna Therapeutics or Novartis' acquisition of Kate Therapeutics, were not included.

Target: company's share price at the previous trading day's close.
Description: For this database, BioPharma Dive looked only at companies developing human medicines. The mergers and acquisitions included in the database are limited by certain criteria, namely that they must be for an entire company — not for product licensings, options to acquire or for partial equity stakes. Additionally, the database is limited to deals valued at a minimum of $50 million upfront. Total consideration can reflect both cash and equity offered upfront in exchange for the acquired company’s shares. Deals in which the upfront payment was less than $50 million or was not specified, like with Eli Lilly's purchase of Versanis and Orna Therapeutics or Novartis' acquisition of Kate Therapeutics, were not included. Premiums were calculated using the acquired company's share price at the previous trading day's close.

Acquirer: Only one therapeutic category is counted for each
Description: Additionally, the database is limited to deals valued at a minimum of $50 million upfront. Total consideration can reflect both cash and equity offered upfront in exchange for the acquired company’s shares. Deals in which the upfront payment was less than $50 million or was not specified, like with Eli Lilly's purchase of Versanis and Orna Therapeutics or Novartis' acquisition of Kate Therapeutics, were not included. Premiums were calculated using the acquired company's share price at the previous trading day's close. Only one therapeutic category is counted for each acquired company. For those with multiple focuses, the chosen therapeutic area is based on lead asset or, lacking one that's distinctly identified, the company is classified under "other."

Acquirer: In some instances, such as the
Description: In some instances, such as the purchase of a private biotech, data for each category either wasn't applicable or wasn't readily available. Finally, BioPharma Dive updates the tracker when deals are announced. Sometimes, transactions fall through or another company outbids the original acquirer. In the former case, we remove the deal from our database; in the latter, we updates entries with the new information. By Gwendolyn Wu • Aug. 9, 2024

Acquirer: Only one therapeutic category is counted for each
Description: Only one therapeutic category is counted for each acquired company. For those with multiple focuses, the chosen therapeutic area is based on lead asset or, lacking one that's distinctly identified, the company is classified under "other." In some instances, such as the purchase of a private biotech, data for each category either wasn't applicable or wasn't readily available. Finally, BioPharma Dive updates the tracker when deals are announced. Sometimes, transactions fall through or another company outbids the original acquirer. In the former case, we remove the deal from our database; in the latter, we updates entries with the new information.

Description: Janes Capital Partners Advises ExoAnalytic Solutions on its Pending Sale to Anduril IRVINE, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Janes Capital Partners announced today that it served as exclusive financial advisor to ExoAnalytic Solutions on its pending acquisition by Anduril Industries. Anduril has signed a definitive agreement to acquire ExoAnalytic Solutions. The deal remains subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals. Headquartered in Foothill Ranch, California, ExoAnalytic Solutions is a leading provider of space domain awareness, missile defense, and space situ

Target: Exacom, a Leading Provider of Cloud-Native Recording and Logging Solutions
Description: Motorola Solutions Acquires Exacom, a Leading Provider of Cloud-Native Recording and Logging Solutions for Mission-Critical 911 and Radio Communications CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) today announced it has acquired Exacom, a leading provider of cloud-native voice and multimedia recording and logging solutions for mission-critical communications, based in Manchester, New Hampshire. Exacom’s solutions help public safety agencies consolidate call logs and record 911 audio and radio traffic directly into their cloud-based or on-premise storage systems, helping to create a single narrative of the entire incident. Agero Enters into Agreement to Acquire Urgently, for $5.50 in Cash Per Share, Expanding Tech-Driven Roadside Assistance Across Automotive, Fleet, Rental, and Insurance Markets MEDFORD, Mass. & ASHBURN, Va.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Agero, Inc., the leading white-label provider of digital driver assistance services and software for major automotive and auto insurance brands, today announced that it has entered into an agreement to acquire Urgent.ly, Inc.
